steps.title: Let's build a Match Pairs quiz slide!
steps.description: A Match Pairs slide challenges participants to connect prompts with answers — great for vocabulary, capitals, dates, and more. Tip: keep each item short and unambiguous so every prompt has exactly one clear answer. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New Presentation' to start a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Match Pairs slide type
steps.description: Select 'Match Pairs' from the slide type picker. You'll find it in the Quiz section — it's the one that turns matching tasks into a scored game.

steps.title: Write your question
steps.description: Type your matching challenge here. Participants will use this as their cue for what to connect.

action.value: Match each country to its capital city
action.hint: Type your question, then click away

steps.title: Enter your first prompt
steps.description: Type the first item your participants must match. Aim for 3–4 pairs — enough to be engaging without crowding a mobile screen. Keep each item a single word or short phrase.

steps.title: Enter the matching answer
steps.description: Now type the answer that goes with your first prompt. The answer column shuffles automatically on the canvas — so participants can't just copy the order.

steps.title: Ready to go live!
steps.description: That's all it takes to build a Match Pairs quiz slide. Notice both columns use the same format — countries left, capitals right — that's parallel structure, and it keeps the pattern clear for participants. Add up to 4 pairs, adjust the time limit and points below, then hit 'Present'.
